mechanics.text:
CHARACTER CREATION
options for minimizing or eliminating the randomness of events_ The most extreme option is to disregard events during char- acter creation_ Ihis does not mean rolling for events and ignoring only the negative ones, however: players should either use the events as described for their characters Or forgo them completely: Alternatively; enterprising players can tailor existing events t0 suit their Own tastes_ If the player has definite story in mind for his character. can trade event that doesn't fit for another event created by the player that affects the character in similar way: This option requires some extra effort, but can be very rewarding
The gamemaster has complete freedom to accept or reject your idea; some concepts will not fit in his campaign Or won' make sense For example it would be difficult (if not impossi: ble) to justify an Inner Sphere White Collar boy suddenly finding himself in Trueborn Sibko After figuring out how your character avoided the prerequi- sites you must pay the price. Your  character' lucky break reduces his or her Edge threshold by 1_ Because no Attribute threshold can drop below 2, this reduction limits the number of times player may avoid prerequisites character' Edge threshold is already 2_ the player may not use these rules
Linda new character, Delilah Morgan, going to bookish technician with knacks for computers and for getting into trouble Linda has pretty good idea of what she wants her character to be like. Starting on the Farm Path (p. 34) in Stage 1, Linda rolls a 8 the event table: Already learning how t0 fix the trac tor This gives Delilah Natural Aptitude with Technician/ICE Skill; which makes Derfect sense; she keeps it Moving on to High School (p. 381, she rolls Local Sports Hero This doesn't really fit her vision of the character at all; but the gamemaster offers her the chance t0 come Up with her Own varia tion on the event instead. She decides on: Win local writing contest: Computers Art/Creative   Writing This event has similar combination Of ore fair Iy useful skill at +1 and one minor skill at +2, and fits the   stage  and the   character; the   gamemaster allows it:
Edge and Events Sometimes a event comes up that you do not want your character experience. In most cases these are negative events: however; sometimes positive event can be undesir- able, depending on your character concept. After rolling dice for an event; you can increase or decrease the die roll result by or reroll; in the same way that you use Edge during game play (see Edge, p. 18). This option allows you t0 avoid catastrophe or angle for positive event: Because your character does not yet have an Edge Attribute, using this option reduces the character" Edge threshold by 1 Unlike dur- ing game play; character may not choose t0 use Edge before making an event roll: As with using Edge t0 avoid prerequisites player may not use this special rule his or her character' Edge threshold is 2.
REPEATING PATHS Characters can repeat paths and subpaths whenever the Next Path option allows repeated path or subpath counts toward character' path limits_ repeated path has sub paths, the character must skip the main path and go directly to one of the subpaths (assuming the character meets the sub path's   prerequisites). For example_ character who had already taken the University Path and Sciences Major Subpath returns to the University Path_ that character would skip direct- Iy t0 another Major Subpath or to the Medical or Field Science School Subpath: Modifiers to character' Attribute minimums and thresh- olds only apply the first time character takes path or sub- path; disregard them o subsequent passes Likewise traits granted by path or subpath are only bestowed once even character follows the same path several times; Traits earned through events are a exception to this rule_ By contrast; character who repeats path or subpath does receive any Skill Points granted by it. Also character repeats path or subpath that awards field, that character receives an additional +3 Skill Points to all the skills in that field_
UBING EDGE IN THE Life PATH Because every player character iS unique individual, play: ers will most Vlkely want to sidestep the standard rules at some point during the Life Path_ Use the following special Edge rules when you want your character to go somewhere that the Life Path technically doesn allow, when the dice   conspire against you_
Edge and Path Restrictions Sometimes character can get into path through sheer Iuck , even he lacks the appropriate prerequisites To do this_ however; the character must use up some luck. You may ignore all the prerequisites for any path; or enter path not listed your Next Path options by following the procedure outlined below First, and most important; come up with story about how your character avoided the prerequisites_ Tell the gamemaster and write into your character' $ history: For example, your character grew Up on the street, but you want her to proceed directly to military school , you will need to explain how she man ages this, since the Military School Path requires the White Collar or Nobility Path:  You might decide that your character befriended ocal mercenary: who turned out t0 be an alumnus of the school in question_ He saw your character' potential and sponsored her for scholarship.
Events and Repeated Paths Multiple passes through the same path can give charac ter excellent skills_ but continuous repetition carries significant risks The second time through Dath or subpath, modify your event roll result by -1. The third time through_ modify it by
